documents.label: A copy of the minor's birth certificate
A copy of your birth certificate
A court order
Foster care certification
A copy of your driver's license or other government-issued identification
A lawfully executed and valid power of attorney
A copy of the minor's Social Security card
A copy of your Social Security card

authority.notes: Listed under 'To show you are the minor's parent or authorized representative' — any one of these is sufficient. The minor's birth certificate appears here as proof of parentage and also under childId.

parentId.notes: Listed under 'For proof of your identity' with 'provide one from each category' — the bullets within this sub-category are OR options.

address.notes: The form collects the guardian's current address as a form field but does not require a separate proof-of-address document.

childId.notes: The form explicitly states 'please provide BOTH of the following' for the minor's documents, making this a true AND requirement.

documents.label: Child's birth certificate
Proof of guardianship (if not named on child's birth certificate)
Driver's license or another government issued identification card (e.g. state ID card)
Child's Social Security card
Proof of address (bank statement, utility bill, or insurance statement)

authority.notes: The child's birth certificate establishes parentage/authority if the parent is named on it. If the parent is not named on the birth certificate, a separate proof of guardianship (court order) is required instead. These are effectively OR alternatives for proving authority.

childId.notes: The page lists both the child's birth certificate and the child's Social Security card as required documents. However, the birth certificate also serves the authority category. Both are listed as separate line items in the 'all of the following' list, suggesting both are required for a complete submission. Setting logic to 'and' to reflect that both are explicitly listed as required.

mailingAddress.notes: Page lists two addresses: standard mail (PO Box 9554) and overnight mail (701 Experian Parkway). The standard mail address is the primary mailing address. Note: this page covers checking if a minor has a credit report, not explicitly a protected consumer freeze — the same address is used for minor-related requests at Experian.

documents.label: A document issued by a federal, state, or local government agency showing proof of parentage, including a birth certificate
An order issued by a court of law
Written communication from a county welfare or probation department certifying the protected consumer is in a foster care setting
A guardianship or conservator document
documents.description: A guardianship or conservator document that is current and not expired, includes the protected consumer's name, and that the name of the person on the court document matches the name of the person submitting the request

documents.label: Copy of a driver's license, state-issued identification card, or any other government-issued identification
A lawfully executed and valid power of attorney
Social Security number or a copy of a Social Security card

authority.notes: The page says 'one piece of documentation' from this list, making these OR options. A birth certificate (birth_child) is listed here as proof of parentage/authority, and also satisfies childId.

parentId.notes: TransUnion groups parent and child identity proof together under a single 'sufficient proof of identification' requirement (step 3), listing SSN/card, birth certificate, and government-issued ID as options for both the parent and the child. Any one of these documents can satisfy the parent identity portion. The same document IDs are reused across parentId and childId since the page does not distinguish separate documents for each person — it says 'confirming your identity AND the identity of the minor' using the same list.

address.notes: The protected consumer freeze instructions do not require a separate proof-of-address document. Address proof is only mentioned as optional for standard adult freeze requests.

childId.notes: TransUnion groups parent and child identity proof together under a single 'sufficient proof of identification' requirement (step 3), listing SSN/card, birth certificate, and government-issued ID as options for both. The same document list applies to both the parent and the child. id_parent is reused here because the page generically says 'government-issued identification' without distinguishing parent vs. child ID documents.

mailingAddress.notes: This is the dedicated mailing address explicitly labeled for 'protected consumer freezes' (minor/incapacitated adult freezes) on both the FAQ page and the address-source page. It is distinct from the standard adult freeze address (P.O. Box 160, Woodlyn, PA 19094).
